commit.message:
fix: enforce blocking hook exit status for Pi Agent (#22)

* fix: line count tracking for file overwrites and blocking hook support

When Pi Agent uses the 'write' tool to overwrite a file, it only sends
the new content without the old content. This caused gryph to treat every
write as a new file creation, missing the lines_removed count.

Changes:
- Read existing file content to calculate proper lines_added/lines_removed
- Use oldText/newText fields (Pi Agent format) for edit tool parsing
- Add buildPayloadForResult to avoid duplicating write details in tool_result
- Skip tool_result for successful file operations to prevent duplicate events
- Add synchronous hook execution with spawnSync for blocking decisions
- Add 30s timeout to prevent agent freeze if hook hangs
- Add test for edit tool with oldText/newText fields

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add line count tracking test script

* fix: address review comments on pi-agent hook handling

- Add error handler on fire-and-forget spawn to prevent crashes
- Fix overwrite-to-empty not counting removed lines
- Fix misleading comment about file_read content capture
- Fix incorrect line count in test comment (2 → 3 lines)
- Trim stderr whitespace in block reason

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: log hook errors and infrastructure failures in pi-agent plugin

- Log exit code 1 (hook error) instead of silently swallowing
- Handle spawnSync failures (ENOENT, EACCES) with logging instead of
  silently collapsing to allow
- Differentiate timeout from other spawn failures in error messages
- Document fail-open design and exit code semantics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Abhisek Datta <abhisek.datta@gmail.com>

* fix: address PR #22 review comments on pi-agent parser

- Define ToolResultContent type for tool_result content instead of []interface{}
- Remove nested type assertions in buildPayloadForResult
- Remove test-line-count.sh script (unit tests cover this)
- Fix overwrite line counting to handle missing filePath
- Fix misleading comments in parser and tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: use CountNewFileLines for new file writes in pi-agent

For new files (target path doesn't exist), use CountNewFileLines
instead of CountDiffLines to avoid the SplitLines("") phantom line
bug. Overwrite tracking preserved via os.ReadFile when file exists.

Adds tests for new file, overwrite, and overwrite-to-empty cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: handle empty string edge cases in pi-agent line counting

Use CountNewFileLines instead of CountDiffLines when either side is
empty to avoid go-difflib SplitLines("") phantom line bug. Remove
dead toolResponse parameter from buildPayload (only called with nil).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

commit.message:
fix(apple-silicon): suppress Rust 2024 unsafe/lint warnings in 23 NEON files (#3265)

## Summary

Eliminates **365 compile errors** when building bitnet-kernels with
`RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings"` on aarch64/Apple Silicon.

### Problem

Rust 2024 edition enforces `unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn` by default,
requiring explicit `unsafe {}` blocks inside `unsafe fn` bodies. All 23
NEON kernel files had a mix of:
- Missing `unsafe {}` around truly unsafe operations (vld1q_f32,
vst1q_f32, ptr::add, raw pointer deref)
- Unnecessary `unsafe {}` around safe NEON intrinsics (vdupq_n_f32,
vaddq_f32, etc.)
- Dead code, unused variables, unused assignments

### Fix


### Verification

```bash
# Before: 365 errors
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu

# After: 0 errors ✅
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo check --workspace --no-default-features --features cpu,full-cli
```

### Files (23)

All in `crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/`:
neon_attention_mask, neon_batch_matmul, neon_conv1d,
neon_dynamic_quantization, neon_embedding_ops, neon_flash_attn_v2,
neon_fma_ops, neon_gelu_ops, neon_group_query_attention,
neon_matmul_tiling, neon_pipeline_scheduler, neon_pooling,
neon_quantized_activation, neon_quantized_ffn, neon_quantized_matmul_v2,
neon_quantized_softmax, neon_residual_ops, neon_sliding_window_attn,
neon_sparse_matmul, neon_token_embedding, neon_vectorized_search,
neon_weight_dequant, simd_attention_mask

### Impact

- **Unblocks Apple Silicon development**: devs can now build with `-D
warnings` on macOS/arm64
- **No behavior change**: only lint suppression, zero functional changes
- **x86_64 neutral**: NEON files are behind `#[cfg(target_arch =
"aarch64")]`, no effect on CI

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Chores**
* Updated internal code quality and compiler configuration across
multiple CPU kernel modules to enhance development stability and
maintainability.

<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

feat(api): add artists list pagination/filter/sort contract (#9) (#169)

* feat(api): add validated artists list query contract (refs #9)

* feat(api): wire artists list/get to sqlite repository (refs #9)

* fix(api): harden artist list sort/pagination pipeline (#171)

* Initial plan

* fix(api): address review feedback on artist list pagination

- Replace `as usize` casts with safe `usize::try_from()` conversions that
  return an empty page on overflow, avoiding potential wrapping on 32-bit
  targets (offset/limit in apply_list_query)
- Replace `sort_by` with `sort_by_cached_key` so name/status sort keys are
  computed once per element rather than on every comparison, and add a UUID
  secondary sort key as a deterministic tie-breaker for stable pagination
- Add four unit tests covering each invalid-input 400 branch: negative
  offset, invalid status, invalid sort_by, invalid sort_order

Closes #169 feedback

Co-authored-by: SvetaKrava <17299577+SvetaKrava@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): sort after bounds check, non-allocating status key, tie-breaker test

- Move total/offset/limit conversion and start>=len early-return before
  the sort so out-of-range requests skip O(n log n) work entirely
- Replace a.status.to_string() in Status sort arm with a u8 rank to
  avoid heap allocation per element (Continuing=0, Ended=1)
- Add apply_query_sort_is_deterministic_when_primary_keys_equal test:
  two artists with same name, known UUIDs from_u128(1/2), submitted in
  both orders; asserts UUID tie-breaker always gives the same result
- Add uuid as dev-dependency for test

Co-authored-by: SvetaKrava <17299577+SvetaKrava@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): import ArtistId in tie-breaker test to fix E0433 compile error

The test `apply_query_sort_is_deterministic_when_primary_keys_equal` used
`ArtistId` but the test module's `use super::*` does not pull in
`chorrosion_domain::ArtistId`. Add an explicit `use chorrosion_domain::ArtistId`
inside the test function to resolve the CI failure.

Co-authored-by: SvetaKrava <17299577+SvetaKrava@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(api): rename ListArtistsQueryError variants to fix clippy::enum_variant_names

Clippy rejects the enum because all variants share the `Invalid` prefix.
Rename variants by removing the prefix: InvalidLimit → Limit, InvalidOffset →
Offset, InvalidStatus → Status, InvalidSortBy → SortBy, InvalidSortOrder →
SortOrder. Update all usages (Display impl, normalize_list_query, tests).

optional, ZodDefault

commit.message:
fix: handle TCP fragmentation for large RPC requests

The TCP RPC handler used a single 8KB read() call, causing large
requests like sendrawtransaction to be truncated. The JSON parser
would fail with 'EOF while parsing a string' because it only received
a partial payload.

Now reads in a loop, parsing Content-Length from HTTP headers to know
when the full request body has been received. Includes a 1MB safety
limit to prevent unbounded memory allocation.

commit.message:
fix: normalize MSYS/Git-bash paths on Windows for cwd override

On Windows CI, bash's $(pwd) returns MSYS-style paths like /d/a/repo
instead of D:/a/repo. These paths are invalid for Go's os/filepath
functions, causing discover.Discover() to fail finding workflow files
and returning allow instead of deny.

Add normalizeMSYSPath() to convert /X/path to X:/path on Windows.

feat: deterministic signature block extraction + dedup protection + anti-hallucination

Key changes:
1. Deterministic signature_block extraction: Parse multiline signature blocks
   to extract org names (Fabrikam Inc., Contoso Ltd.) via regex instead of LLM.
   Eliminates LLM non-determinism for proper noun entities in signature blocks.

2. Dedup protection for deterministic entities: Tag entities from deterministic
   extraction (signature_block, signature_party, letterhead) and prevent them
   from being merged away during entity dedup. Fixes catastrophic transitive
   merge chain (fabrikam inc -> contoso -> ... -> 3000 00) that caused proper
   noun entities to disappear.

3. Anti-hallucination prompt Rule 7: 'Entity names MUST come from the sentence
   text — do NOT invent or hallucinate names not present in the source.'
   Prevents LLM from generating entities like 'outdoor' that don't appear in text.

4. Route 6 fix: Remove stale 'chunk' variable from Cypher WITH clause after
   chunk_id removal.

Root cause of the dedup cascade: Voyage voyage-context-3 embeddings of short
entity names have high cosine similarity (fabrikam inc ↔ contoso ltd = 0.81),
causing transitive Union-Find clustering to merge unrelated entities at 0.8
threshold. Deterministic entities now carry metadata.deterministic=True and
are excluded from merge_map application.

* Claude/evaluate owasp zap 5azc3 (#290)

* Improve curl error handling in Observatory workflow

Add set -euo pipefail for general robustness and an explicit
|| { echo "::error::..." } trap on the curl command. Previously,
a failing curl left result empty and the subsequent jq call
emitted a cryptic parse error; now a clear GitHub Actions error
annotation is shown instead.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EgASw7CgVmzAgjQcVoqVyS

* Guard against null grade from Observatory API

When the API returns an error response (rate-limit, scan pending,
unexpected payload), jq emits the string "null" for .grade, which
previously matched the wildcard case with a misleading "grade null
is below minimum" error. Now an explicit check catches null or empty
grade and emits a distinct error with the raw API response for easier
diagnosis.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EgASw7CgVmzAgjQcVoqVyS

* Distinguish transport errors from HTTP errors in Observatory curl

Previously, curl -sf would exit non-zero on both network failures and
non-2xx HTTP responses (e.g. 429, 503), producing the same misleading
"curl failed to reach Observatory API" message in all cases.

Switch to curl -s (no --fail), capture the HTTP status code via
-w "%{http_code}" into a dedicated variable, and check it explicitly
before parsing the JSON body. This produces distinct error messages:
- Transport error (DNS/timeout): "curl transport error ... (network failure or timeout)"
- HTTP error: "Observatory API returned HTTP <code>: <body>"

The response body is written to a mktemp file via -o so that the
-w status suffix doesn't pollute the JSON payload.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EgASw7CgVmzAgjQcVoqVyS

* Validate JSON before jq to catch non-JSON API responses

With set -euo pipefail, a non-JSON body (HTML error page, proxy
intercept, etc.) would cause jq to exit non-zero and terminate the
step without any ::error:: annotation — just a raw jq parse error.

Add a `jq empty` check after the HTTP status guard. jq empty exits 0
on valid JSON and non-zero otherwise; 2>/dev/null suppresses jq's own
parse noise so only our annotation reaches the log. The error message
includes the HTTP status code and up to 300 chars of the raw response
for immediate diagnosis.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EgASw7CgVmzAgjQcVoqVyS

* Clean up mktemp temp file on all exit paths

The result_file created by mktemp was never removed, leaving a stray
/tmp/tmp.* file on the runner whenever the step exited early (transport
error, non-200 HTTP, invalid JSON, or bad grade).

Register a trap on EXIT immediately after mktemp so the file is always
removed regardless of how the shell exits — normal completion, explicit
exit 1, or set -e termination.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01EgASw7CgVmzAgjQcVoqVyS

* Claude/fix observatory scan polling 4 jun i (#293)

* fix(ci): add polling and retry/backoff to Observatory workflow

- POST to /api/v2/scan once to trigger the scan, then poll
  GET /api/v2/analyze until a grade is returned, avoiding the race
  where a pending scan (grade: null) caused an immediate exit 1
- Poll up to 18 times with 10s sleep (3-minute window); emit a clear
  timeout error if the scan never completes
- Wrap all curl calls in retry_curl() with up to 5 attempts and
  exponential backoff (5→10→20→40→80s) for transport errors and 5xx
- Handle HTTP 429 rate-limit responses separately with a fixed 60s wait
  before retrying, rather than treating them as hard failures

Fixes flaky Monday scheduled runs caused by async scan results and
transient API unavailability unrelated to the site's security headers.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013ivndqABtzhjjq6a68aioh

* fix(readme): fix Status table header/separator column mismatch

The header had 3 columns (| | |) but the separator only had 2 (| --- | --- |),
breaking Markdown table rendering. Changed to a proper 2-column table
with | Item | Status | headers to match the separator and data rows.

https://claude.ai/code/session_013ivndqABtzhjjq6a68aioh

* fix(observatory): initialize http_code/result and fix stale comment

- Initialize http_code="000" and result="" before the retry loop in
  retry_curl so that set -u never sees an unbound variable when every
  curl attempt is a transport error (the error-path continue branch
  never assigned http_code, causing the final ::error:: message to
  terminate the script via set -u before it could print)
- Update the MAX_POLL_ATTEMPTS comment: the old "18 × 10s = 3 minutes"
  was wrong because each poll can also spend time inside retry_curl
  (exponential backoff plus up to 60s per 429); replace with a comment
  that reflects the actual upper bound

https://claude.ai/code/session_013ivndqABtzhjjq6a68aioh

commit.message:
Fix 'Current Location' navigation bug — resolve GPS at navigation level

Root cause: GPS resolution was tied to CurrentScreen via a boolean flag
(pendingUseDeviceLocation) with race conditions. Selecting 'Current
Location' from Forecast/Maps would navigate away to the Current tab,
and the flag could be consumed before GPS completed.

Fix: Move GPS resolution to a new NavigationViewModel accessible from
ZephyrusNavHost. When 'Current Location' is selected from search:
1. NavigationViewModel resolves GPS via LocationRepository (cached)
2. Shared coordinates are updated directly
3. navController.popBackStack() returns to the SAME screen
4. The screen sees the new coordinates and refreshes

This eliminates the flag-based signaling, the tab-switch side effect,
and all race conditions. Works identically from Current, Forecast,
and Maps screens.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
